name: mimg
description: A chained image modifier written in Zig
readme:
# mimg

A high-performance command-line image processing tool written in Zig.

**[View Documentation & Gallery](https://mimg.brian-sinquin.fr)**

## Features

- Fast image processing with support for multiple formats (PNG, JPEG, BMP, QOI)
- Chainable modifiers for complex transformations
- Built-in presets for common operations
- Comprehensive set of filters and effects
- Zero external dependencies

## Installation

### Build from Source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/brian-sinquin/mimg.git
cd mimg
zig build
```

The compiled binary will be available at `zig-out/bin/mimg`.

### Requirements

- Zig 0.15.1 or later

## Usage

### Basic Syntax

```bash
mimg <input> [modifiers...] [-o output]
```

### Examples

```bash
# Adjust brightness
mimg input.png brightness 20 -o output.png

# Chain multiple modifiers
mimg input.png grayscale blur 5 -o output.png

# Use a preset
mimg input.png preset vintage -o output.png
```

## Documentation

- [Installation Guide](docs/installation.md)
- [Available Modifiers](docs/modifiers.md)
- [Usage Examples](docs/examples.md)
- [Presets](docs/presets.md)
- [Supported Formats](docs/formats.md)

## Development

### Build the Website

Generate the documentation website with gallery examples:

```bash
zig build website
```

Output will be in `website/zig-out/`.

### Run Tests

```bash
zig build test
```

## License

MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

## Links

- [Documentation](https://mimg.brian-sinquin.fr)
- [Gallery](https://mimg.brian-sinquin.fr/gallery)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/brian-sinquin/mimg)

releases.name: Release v0.1.4
releases.body:
# mimg v0.1.4 Release Notes

**Release Date:** 2025-10-15

## Highlights

- Major performance improvements across image resizing, gradients, and color operations.
- Optimized core math and filter routines for faster processing.
- Expanded filter capabilities and improved code organization.
- All existing commands and APIs remain fully compatible.

## Verification

- All tests and builds pass.
- No breaking changes.

releases.name: Release v0.1.3
releases.body:
# Release Notes

## Version 0.1.3

### Improvements
- Added progress indicators for long-running operations
- Improved memory usage with better buffer management
- Enhanced build system optimizations

### Technical Changes
- Progress bars for batch processing operations
- Memory optimization for image processing pipeline
- Better buffer reuse in filter operations

---

*For detailed documentation, see [docs/](docs/)*

releases.name: Release v0.1.2
releases.body:
# Release Notes - v0.1.2

## SIMD Performance Optimization

This release introduces comprehensive SIMD vectorization improvements for enhanced image processing performance.

### Key Improvements
- **Complete SIMD refactoring** of all color adjustment functions using proper `@Vector(4, f32)` and `@Vector(4, u8)` operations
- **New SIMD utilities module** (`src/simd_utils.zig`) with centralized vector operations and pixel processing helpers
- **Eliminated manual 4-pixel chunking** and replaced with efficient vectorized operations
- **Maintained type safety** while significantly improving performance

### Refactored Functions
- `adjustBrightness`, `adjustContrast`, `adjustSaturation`
- `adjustGamma`, `adjustExposure`, `adjustVibrance`
- `invertColors`, `grayscaleImage`, `applySepia`
- All functions now use proper SIMD vectorization

### Performance Impact
- Improved throughput for color adjustments and effects
- Reduced code duplication and improved maintainability
- Better memory efficiency with vectorized operations

### Version 0.1.2 Core Features
- ✅ Gather input image from URL
- ✅ Check first arg is a file path then assume URL, else error
- ✅ Load batch of images using wildcards with multithreading
- ✅ Use preset files by dispatching each line's chain on input
- ✅ Better help message
- ✅ Improve error handling
- ✅ Add unit tests
- ✅ File extension conversion

## Technical Details
- **Zig Version**: 0.15.1
- **SIMD Operations**: `@Vector(4, f32)` and `@Vector(4, u8)` for 4-pixel parallel processing
- **Memory Management**: Automatic buffer reuse and tiled processing for large images
- **Compatibility**: All existing APIs and command-line interfaces preserved

## Testing
- ✅ All unit tests pass
- ✅ Batch processing validated
- ✅ Gallery generation works correctly
- ✅ No compilation errors or warnings

---

*For more information, see [README.md](README.md) and [performance benchmarks](README.md#performance).*"

releases.name: Release v0.1.1
releases.body:
# mimg v0.1.1

## Changes

- Fixed saturation algorithm (HSL → luminance-based interpolation)
- Fixed hue-shift algorithm (HSL → RGB)
- Simplified gallery template
- Cleaner build scripts
- New modifiers
- Improved README

---

releases.name: First release v0.1.0
releases.body:
# mimg v0.1.0

Initial release of mimg, a command-line image manipulation tool built with Zig.

## Features

- **18 image processing modifiers**: invert, resize, crop, rotate, flip, grayscale, brightness, contrast, saturation, gamma, blur, gaussian-blur, sharpen, emboss, sepia, vignette, posterize, hue-shift, median-filter
- **Modifier chaining**: Apply multiple effects in sequence
- **Gallery system**: 29 example images with `zig build gallery`
- **Cross-platform**: Binaries for Linux, macOS, Windows (x86_64, ARM, RISC-V, i386)
- **Silent operation**: No output by default, use `--verbose` for details

## Usage

```bash
# Single modifier
mimg image.png invert --output result.png

# Chain modifiers
mimg image.png brightness 20 contrast 1.5 sharpen --output enhanced.png

# Generate gallery
zig build gallery
```

## Installation

Download binaries from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/yourusername/mimg/releases).

Or build from source:
```bash
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseSafe
```

## Special Thanks

Lena Söderberg for the iconic test image.

---

See gallery.md for complete documentation and examples.

owner_company: Max Born Institute for Nonlinear Optics and Short Pulse Spectroscopy
